title: Lloyds Semantic Evaluation Dataset
purpose: Test whether Flora can understand substantive content from governed Evidence and Observations without relying on pre-authored conclusions.

source_documents.title: Lloyds Banking Group Annual Report and Accounts 2025
Lloyds Banking Group Q1 2026 Interim Management Statement
Lloyds accelerates AI innovation with Google Cloud
UK financial system strengthened with new safeguards for major technology providers

passages.content: Lloyds reported 28m customers, 23.6m digitally active customers, £496.5bn customer deposits and £481.1bn underlying loans at 2025 year-end.
Lloyds reported around 21.5m app users, with app use up about 45% since 2021.
Lloyds reported a seven-minute mobile current-account opening process that drove around 85% of current-account openings in 2025.

Lloyds said the structural hedge covers net liabilities that are stable or less rate-sensitive; Q1 2026 hedge balance was £246bn and the increase reflected strong hedge-eligible balances including personal current accounts.
Lloyds reported £1.6bn income from structural hedge balances in Q1 2026, versus £1.2bn in Q1 2025, and expected structural hedge earnings above £7bn in 2026 and above £8bn in 2027.

Lloyds reported £1.9bn gross cost savings since 2021, c.45% higher active customers served per distribution FTE, c.30% gross reduction in run and change technology costs and a 2026 cost:income target below 50%.

Lloyds linked strategic investment to technology, data and people, including 9,000 tech and data hires, Copilot licences, AI foundations, mobile app and digital journey investment and legacy app/cloud migration priorities.

Lloyds said Halifax customers will start to use the Lloyds app, accounts will be rebranded over time, sort codes and account numbers will stay the same, and FSCS protection for existing Halifax balances remains separate from new Lloyds accounts.
Lloyds said it is using Google Cloud Vertex AI, has over 300 data scientists and AI developers using the platform, and has migrated 15 modelling systems from on-premise infrastructure to Vertex AI.

HM Treasury designated Microsoft, Google Cloud, AWS and Oracle as Critical Third Parties from 13 July 2026, with Bank of England, PRA and FCA oversight of critical services they provide to the financial sector.

Public evidence shows digital activity, app usage and current-account openings, but does not prove primary-account strengthening, salary-flow capture, direct-debit concentration or challenger displacement.
Lloyds forward-looking risk language includes operational risks from third-party suppliers, AI, cyber, technological failure and inadequate or failed internal or external processes or systems.

derived_evidence.claim: Lloyds had large customer, deposit and loan scale at 2025 year-end.
Lloyds had high app usage and most 2025 current-account openings flowed through the mobile journey.
Stable or less-rate-sensitive net liabilities, including personal current accounts, were associated with a larger structural hedge and higher hedge income.
Lloyds reported simplification, technology-cost reduction and investment activity, but public passages do not identify internal capital allocation governance.
Halifax will migrate brand/app presentation toward Lloyds while some customer identifiers and protection arrangements stay unchanged.
Lloyds uses Google Cloud AI services, and Google Cloud is within the UK Critical Third Party perimeter for financial-sector critical services.

observations.statement: Digital engagement evidence supports channel activity and acquisition efficiency, not primary-account primacy by itself.
The deposit/structural-hedge/NII evidence is a Lloyds-specific commercial mechanism candidate because the passages connect personal current accounts, hedge balances and hedge income.
The cloud/AI evidence creates both transformation evidence and supplier-control uncertainty; sector CTP designation should be related to Lloyds only through its Google Cloud usage.

unknowns.statement: Salary-flow capture, direct-debit concentration and main-bank status are not evidenced.
unknowns.evidence_demand: Comparable Lloyds transaction-flow or primary-bank data.

unknowns.statement: Internal allocation of NII/capital between transformation, distributions, buffers and growth is not visible in the selected passages.
unknowns.evidence_demand: Board/investment governance or programme funding evidence.

unknowns.statement: Core-system map, workload criticality, outage root causes and migration sequencing are not exposed.
unknowns.evidence_demand: Technology estate and resilience evidence below public-report granularity.

candidate_contradictions.proposition_a: Halifax customers will move toward the Lloyds app and Lloyds branding.
candidate_contradictions.proposition_b: Halifax account numbers, sort codes and existing FSCS protection arrangements stay unchanged.
candidate_contradictions.expected_status: not-a-contradiction; different aspects of identity and operational continuity

candidate_contradictions.proposition_a: Lloyds reports AI/cloud migration and simplification activity.
candidate_contradictions.proposition_b: Lloyds also discloses operational risks from suppliers, AI, cyber and technology failure.
candidate_contradictions.expected_status: tension, not contradiction; activity can increase capability while preserving risk
